headline: GOLD RETREATS TO $4,710 AS BULLS PAUSE AFTER 50% ANNUAL SURGE; BANK TARGETS STILL ABOVE $5,000
snippet: Gold pulled back to $4,709.64/oz on April 26, easing from highs above $4,800 earlier this month. The metal remains up more than 50% year-over-year, supported by record central bank buying and flat mine supply. J.P. Morgan targets $5,055/oz by Q4 2026; Goldman Sachs holds a $4,900 base with upside to $5,000+.

headline: GOLDSEEK COT REPORT APRIL 24: GOLD +2.8%, SILVER SURGES 5.38% — SIL ETF BREAKS KEY RESISTANCE
snippet: The April 24 CFTC Commitments of Traders data shows gold up 2.8% and silver surging 5.38% in the reporting period on a weaker US dollar and escalating trade tensions. Silver miners, tracked by the SIL ETF, rallied 6.56% and finally broke above the key $38–$43 resistance zone that had capped the sector.

headline: GOLDSEEK ASIAN METALS MARKET UPDATE APRIL 24: Gold Holds Near $4,700 as China Restocking Lifts Base Metals
snippet: GoldSeek's daily Asian metals market update for April 24, 2026, covering overnight price action in gold, silver, and base metals. Chinese restocking ahead of the May 1–5 Labor Day holiday provided a demand floor for copper and industrial metals in Asian session trading.

headline: CONTANGO SILVER & GOLD OUTLINES $63M ALASKA DRILL PROGRAM — 18,000M AT LUCKY SHOT NEAR ANCHORAGE
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold has released one of the largest junior exploration budgets this cycle: $63M across Alaska and northern BC, anchored by an 18,000m drill campaign at Lucky Shot — a three-portal historic underground gold project about 112 road miles north of Anchorage. The program targets resource expansion at the Coleman, Lucky Shot, and War Baby zones.

headline: ELORO RESOURCES: 350M+ OZ SILVER AT ISKA ISKA IN BOLIVIA — TIN ENDOWMENT MORE THAN DOUBLES IN UPDATED MRE
snippet: Eloro Resources (TSX-V: ELO) delivered a major resource growth update at its Iska Iska silver-tin project in Bolivia: 358M+ oz silver total (109.5M oz indicated + 248.6M oz inferred), with contained tin up 107.7% to 290,000 tonnes. Total inferred tonnage is up 41% versus the 2023 initial MRE. A 40,000m 2026 drill program will feed a PEA.

headline: MORGAN STANLEY DOWNGRADES FREEPORT AFTER Q1 BEAT — GRASBERG MUD RISK CLOUDS COPPER OUTLOOK
snippet: Despite Freeport-McMoRan posting strong Q1 2026 earnings ($881M net income, copper at $5.78/lb avg), Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock on April 24, citing the Grasberg mud rush that cut Indonesia copper sales from 290M lbs to 82M lbs year-over-year. FCX revised 2026 guidance down to 3.1B lbs copper.

headline: DISCOVERY METALS POSTS 60,269 OZ GOLD IN Q1 2026 — PORCUPINE OPS SOLID, OWL CREEK EXPANSION DRILLING IN
snippet: Discovery Metals produced 60,269 oz of gold in Q1 2026 at its Porcupine Operations in Ontario, kicking off the year on a solid footing. Exploration results also arrived from district drilling at Owl Creek and the Broulan Pit, alongside initial drilling at the Dome and TVZ targets.

headline: URANIUM AT $86.80/LB, UP 30% YEAR-ON-YEAR — NEXGEN TO BREAK GROUND ON ROOK MINE IN NORTHERN SASK. THIS SUMMER
snippet: Uranium spot eased 0.40% to $86.80/lb on April 24 but remains 30% above year-ago levels as utility under-contracting reverses. NexGen Energy confirmed it will start construction on the Rook deposit this summer — Canada's largest development-stage uranium project in northern Saskatchewan. Uranium equities are up an average 40% year-to-date.

headline: CHINA SILVER IMPORTS EXCEED 790 TONNES IN EARLY 2026 — SOLAR DEMAND FORCES SIXTH STRAIGHT SUPPLY DEFICIT
snippet: China imported over 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026, driven primarily by solar panel manufacturing. Combined with the Silver Institute's projected 67M oz supply deficit, the silver market is running its sixth consecutive structural shortfall. Primary silver miners' global average AISC sits around $12.21/oz versus spot near $40 — record margins.

headline: USA RARE EARTH BIDS $2.8B FOR BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE — SOLE WESTERN PRODUCER OF ALL 4 MAGNETIC RARE EARTHS
snippet: USA Rare Earth announced a $2.8B acquisition of Brazil's Serra Verde (Pela Ema mine), described as the only producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale. The cash-and-shares deal targets Q3 2026 close and is a direct strategic challenge to China's dominance of the global rare earth supply chain.

headline: RARE EARTH PRICES SURGE IN APRIL: TERBIUM +20.7%, NdPr AT $126/KG — UP 138% YEAR-TO-DATE
snippet: Rare earth prices surged through April 2026, with terbium jumping 20.7% and neodymium-praseodymium (NdPr) climbing to $126/kg — up 138% year-to-date. A second consecutive annual supply deficit and global EV demand forecast at 22.9 million units are underpinning the structural move in magnet metals.

headline: GOLD STOCKS STILL UNDERVALUED IN 'STRUCTURAL BULL' — BMO NAMES ARIS, DISCOVERY SILVER, MONTAGE, NEWMONT
snippet: Despite gold's 50%+ annual surge, mining equities remain at compressed multiples relative to cash flow — a disconnect analysts argue cannot last. BMO's top sector picks include Aris Mining, Discovery Silver, Montage Gold, and Newmont, with the thesis that valuations must eventually catch up to the metal's historic run.
